Film-maker Alex Gibney was initially commissioned to follow the cyclist as he prepared to make a professional comeback by competing in the Tour de France, only to find himself documenting an unprecedented scandal as Armstrong was exposed as having used performance-enhancing drugs.

programme.title: I Am Joe Frazier
programme.desc: A profile of the former world heavyweight boxing champion, a perfectionist who triumphantly emerged from Muhammad Ali's long shadow.

programme.title: Albert Brooks: Defending My Life
programme.desc: A profile of the actor, comedian and film-maker, including interviews with Sharon Stone, Larry David, James L Brooks, Conan O'Brien, Sarah Silverman and Jonah Hill.

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: The life and work of American film-maker and choreographer director Stanley Donen, best known for co-directing On the Town and Singin' in the Rain with Gene Kelly.

programme.title: Discovering: Alan Rickman
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of the English actor, who starred in Die Hard, Robin Hood: Prince of Thieves and Love Actually, as well as the Harry Potter franchise.

programme.title: Charlie Hustle & the Matter of Pete Rose
programme.desc: A look at how Rose, after admitting to betting on baseball, sought reinstatement two decades later, making a final bid for redemption in the sport he loves.

programme.title: Discovering: Octavia Spencer
programme.desc: The life and career of the American actress, whose films include The Shape of Water, Fruitvale Station, Hidden Figures, and The Help, for which she won an Academy Award.

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: The life and work of George Stevens, who directed films including Shane, The Greatest Story Ever Told and The Diary of Anne Frank and won Oscars for A Place in the Sun and Giant.

programme.title: Kim's Video
programme.desc: Documentary about a video and music retail store in Manhattan, New York City, that inspired a generation of cinephiles before it mysteriously closed its doors and sent its film archive to a small Sicilian village for safekeeping.

programme.title: My Icon: Ebony Rainford-Brent
programme.desc: The former England cricket star and World Cup winner discusses the individuals that have inspired her in her life and career.

programme.title: The Eighties
programme.desc: A look at the events of Ronald Reagan's two terms in the White House, examining landmark legislative achievements as well as political setbacks.

programme.title: Paul Simon: In Restless Dreams
programme.desc: First of a two-part documentary exploring the 60-year career of the singer-songwriter, from his partnership with Art Garfunkel to the global success of his 1986 LP Graceland, as he returns to the studio to record his 2023 album Seven Psalms.

programme.title: Paul Simon: In Restless Dreams
programme.desc: Conclusion of the documentary exploring the 60-year career of the singer-songwriter, looking at his return to the studio to record his 2023 album Seven Psalms.

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: The life and work of George Stevens, who directed films including Shane, The Greatest Story Ever Told and The Diary of Anne Frank and won Oscars for A Place in the Sun and Giant.

programme.title: Discovering: Octavia Spencer
programme.desc: The life and career of the American actress, whose films include The Shape of Water, Fruitvale Station, Hidden Figures, and The Help, for which she won an Academy Award.

programme.title: After the Bite
programme.desc: Documentary investigating the surge in great white sharks patrolling Cape Cod's shallow waters and the fallout from one young man's death.

programme.title: My Icon: Thierry Henry
programme.desc: The former Arsenal and France forward speaks about the individuals that have been an inspiration to him over the course of his career.

programme.title: God Bless Ozzy Osbourne
programme.desc: A profile of Black Sabbath member Ozzy Osbourne, from the wild and hedonistic days that adversely affected his personal life, through the years of depression, alcoholism and drug dependency that followed. The programme also reveals how he overcame his demons, with the help of his second wife Sharon. Featuring interviews with Paul McCartney, Motley Crue drummer Tommy Lee and others.

programme.title: The Essex Murders
programme.desc: Documentary about the killing of three notorious criminals known as the Essex Boys, who were found shot to death in December 1995. Friends and fellow gangsters reveal how the gang and their leader Tony Tucker managed to build a drug empire as the rave scene took off.

programme.title: The Essex Murders
programme.desc: A look at whether Michael Steele and Jack Whomes were really guilty of the Essex Boys murders, re-examining the questions that remain about their convictions.

programme.title: The Essex Murders
programme.desc: A look at the hunt for an alternative suspect in the Essex Boys murders, uncovering explosive revelations that could turn the case on its head.

programme.title: Liverpool Narcos
programme.desc: Documentary about the drugs trade in 1980s Liverpool, which turned dealing into a billion-pound business and changed Britain forever.

programme.title: Africa's Hunters
programme.desc: A litter of wild dogs comes under threat from a pack of hyenas that moves into their territory - and a female pup that is a loner among her siblings is the most at risk.

programme.title: Gangs of Lemur Island
programme.desc: The lemurs have fought hard to keep their troops healthy and their territories intact, but as their babies grow, the hierarchy starts to change.

programme.title: Undiscovered Vistas
programme.desc: Zion National Park in Utah, one of the world's most spectacular and unforgiving natural landscapes - a 100,000-square-mile labyrinth of deep canyons and intimidating walls.

programme.title: Extreme Africa
programme.desc: Tanzania's Lake Manyara National Park, an area enriched by ancient volcanos which provides a variety of habitats for life to thrive.

programme.title: The Island of Giants
programme.desc: Exploring how whale sharks drive the culture of Santa Maria, the only European hotspot for these giants and why warming seas may be drawing them in.

programme.title: Great Blue Wild
programme.desc: The Belize Barrier Reef is the second-largest coral reef in the world, and its waters are home to a remarkable marine ecosystem that includes rare and exotic fish.

programme.title: Arabian Inferno
programme.desc: Life around the wadis, small waterholes in the desert and vital resources for the creatures that live there. However, danger is never far away as predators join the party.

programme.title: Undiscovered Vistas
programme.desc: Arizona's Paria Plateau, a true geological marvel, consisting of sweeping canyons bound by towering cliffs and fossilised dunes striped in desert red.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Woolly monkey Oriana's health check causes concern, the chimps have a bust-up and the guenon family try out a new tree in their enclosure.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: A video of a sick male marmoset results in an urgent rescue mission. It may be too late to save the male, but there's hope yet for a female at the same address.

programme.title: Great Blue Wild
programme.desc: An underwater cave system hidden in the crystal clear waters of Cozumel in the Caribbean.

programme.title: Arabian Inferno
programme.desc: The Arabian Peninsula, the world's transit hub for flocks of birds migrating between Europe, Africa and Asia, which has billions of flying visitors every year.

programme.title: Land of Primates
programme.desc: A profile of the gelada, a rare monkey that lives in Ethiopia's Simien Mountains. They are charismatic, vocal and prone to mixing affection and confrontation.

programme.title: Africa's Hunters
programme.desc: A buffalo herd moves into a lion pride's territory in search of water - but may end up as prey to the hungry big cats, who bide their time waiting to strike.

programme.title: The Kimberley: Australia's Wild West
programme.desc: A look at Australia's Wild West - The Kimberley - the animals who live there and tracking the quests of those working to protect them.

programme.title: Wildlife ER
programme.desc: Vet Karina fights to save a koala mauled by dogs. Plus, rescue Chantel tries to pick up a peeved possum, while vet Meaghan bonds with a gaggle of goslings.

programme.title: Fixer Upper
programme.desc: Andrew and Brittany are first-time buyers; they're looking for a home with plenty of space for their beloved pooches.

programme.title: Fixer Upper
programme.desc: Chip and Joanna have the challenge of designing a living space that is move-in ready within two months for friend, fellow designer and impending mother Kristen Bufton.

programme.title: Fixer Upper
programme.desc: The grand opening of the silos arrives; Chip and Jo look for someone to run the new Magnolia Bed & Breakfast, and find a retirement-age couple who may be the perfect candidates for the job.

programme.title: Fixer Upper
programme.desc: Chip and Jo work their magic for a family with two recently adopted girls.

programme.title: Fixer Upper
programme.desc: A single foster mother wants to find the ideal environment to raise children, and gets help from her twin sister for the decision.

programme.title: Fixer Upper
programme.desc: Empty nesters Kelvin and Culetta want a home for their children and grandchildren, and get help from their professional football player son.

programme.title: Fixer Upper
programme.desc: Chip and Jo work their magic for a family with two recently adopted girls.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid
programme.desc: A Colorado couple attempts to build one of the most energy-efficient homesteads in the state; Mother Nature and bleeding-edge technologies give them a run for their money as they race to complete their high-end alpine lodge before winter hits.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid
programme.desc: A couple wants to build a country cottage on their remote family farm in southeast Georgia despite the fact that building that kind of structure in four months will be anything but easy; the novice builders face material delays, hurricanes and wildlife.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid
programme.desc: Marine scientists in Florida hire engineers Nicolas Derouin and Arnaud Luguet to create a first-of-its-kind floating home; they have nine months to complete it, but unpredictable weather and building hiccups may sink this project before it can swim.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: A museum in Townsend, Tenn., enlists the Barnwood Builders to restore a 19th-century gunsmith's cabin to be the centerpiece of an exhibit; after repairing the timbers, the team faces the challenge of stacking them flush with a 9-foot ceiling.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: "The Barnwood Builders" return to Lake Lure, N.C., to rebuild Russel's Fort, a historic 1770s log cabin they salvaged months before; the fort now has a gorgeous view of the lake, but the build is full of challenges.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: The guys head to Albertville, Ala., to build a log cabin guest house high atop Sand Mountain; Mark visits a finished cabin he and the guys built on Lake Guntersville; Sherman and Johnny visit a woodturner who makes handcrafted wooden tops.

programme.title: Ranch to Table
programme.desc: Elizabeth makes pear and Gorgonzola pizza as well as a rustic garbanzo bean soup with chili oil.

programme.title: Ranch to Table
programme.desc: Elizabeth wants to celebrate a close friend who recently had twins; to give her a chance to relax, Elizabeth prepares a roasted cherry tomato tart and chocolate chip cookies for brunch on the ranch.

programme.title: Ranch to Table
programme.desc: It's lavender season on the ranch; Elizabeth's friends and family help harvest the lavender field behind her parents' house; as a thank you, Elizabeth makes chicken piri piri and lemon cake with lemon curd buttercream frosting.

programme.title: Ranch to Table
programme.desc: Elizabeth Poett welcomes us to her family's 14,000-acre ranch in California; working side by side with her husband, Austin, she tends to the land beginning at dawn; for dinner, she prepares a Santa Maria Style Tri-Tip and a Blackberry-Apple Galette.

programme.title: Ranch to Table
programme.desc: Life as a rancher and a mom can be busy, so Elizabeth invites close friends and fellow moms to the ranch for a well-deserved night off; she prepares ribeye steaks and serves a walnut cake with burnt butter cream frosting for dessert.

programme.title: Ranch to Table
programme.desc: Elizabeth spends the day working cattle with her father at the historic Red Corrals; later, she hosts her parents for dinner and prepares some of their favorites, including arroz con pollo and lemon meringue pie.

programme.desc: Two brothers and their wives look for the perfect vacation home in Door County, Wisconsin, for their extended family of 30.

programme.title: Lakefront Bargain Hunt: Renovation

programme.title: Lakefront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: A Nashville couple wants a waterfront retreat on Alabama's Smith Lake to escape their hectic lives; they may have to take on major renovations to create space where they can make memories with their kids and entertain friends and family.

programme.title: Lakefront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: College sweethearts are ready to find a vacation home for their family on Alabama's Smith Lake; they're shown several properties but each will require some work.

programme.title: Lakefront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: A blended family of eight finds a vacation home on Lake Martin, Ala.; the property needs major renovations, but he's a contractor and is ready to get to work to help the family realize their lakeside living dreams.

programme.title: Lakefront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: Two couples team up to buy their dream vacation house on Deep Creek Lake, Md.; their agreed upon must-haves include water views and space for nine people but finding a property that both couples agree on may be a challenge.

programme.title: Lakefront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: Minnesota is known as the Land of 10,000 Lakes and there's no better place to find the perfect house on the perfect lake; with lakefront prices at an all-time high, this couple will need to create their dream retreat with some hands-on renovations.
